title: LIVE R3 incident: ETH/BTC pair auto-guard had wrong z-window + crypto close not chunked (both fixed, committed bf4d8a6)
source: Live R3 manual pair-trade session 2026-06-24 (operator + Claude)
why: During live R3, after stopping the survival service to trade manually, an ETH/BTC dollar-neutral pair (long ETH 63 / short BTC 1.67) was opened. Two execution bugs surfaced in the new operator tooling: (1) pair_guard.py computed the H1 z-score on a 96-bar window while pair_monitor_ethbtc.py uses 168 bars -> different z; the guard read -2.64 while the monitor displayed deeper, so it did NOT fire at the -3 the operator was watching. (2) When realigned to 168 the guard fired at z=-3.21 but the single-order close left a naked leg: the venue fills crypto closes PARTIALLY (a 1.67-lot BTC close filled ~1.0) and rejected the rest (retcode 10006), leaving ETH long 5 + BTC short 1.67 until cleaned up by hand with a re-read/retry loop. Same bug class as B0211 equity-lock chunking.
value_rationale: Execution-reliability fix for live capital tooling; prevents a stop leaving a naked directional leg. Low effort, medium value (only matters while trading crypto manually).
scope: FIXED in commit bf4d8a6: pair_guard H1_WIN=96->168 to match the monitor; shared robust close_all_legs/close_position in pair_close.py that re-reads remaining volume, chunks by volume_max, retries partial fills + transient rejects (10006/10039); pair_guard imports it (single source of truth). Both scripts compile clean, guard imports OK, dry-run verified flat.
recommendation: Tools now reliable for crypto. Before next autonomous use, get a live end-to-end test of the chunked close with an actual open pair (could not test while flat). Keep z-window in sync between monitor and any future guard.

history.reason: Live R3 execution incident recorded per CLAUDE.md mandate (log live incidents at the moment). Pair experiment realized ~-3994 USD; account flat at 1,000,342 (+342 over 1M start), still #51 above the cut.
